safety_requirements.logical_block: corresponds to customer requirement SDS-EV-310 and belongs to milestone PF3.0.
safety_requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC application shall support the following operating states:
safety_requirements.inputs: The input conditions include ignition status, CAN commands, HVIL state, fault conditions, and battery voltage measurements.
safety_requirements.outputs: The outputs include state machine status broadcasts and power module control signals.
safety_requirements.process: The software shall transition between states only when all safety conditions are satisfied.

metadata.source_section: corresponds to customer requirement SDS-EV-310 and belongs to milestone PF3.0.
metadata.evidence:
SWRS_STATE_001 corresponds to customer requirement SDS-EV-310 and belongs to milestone PF3.0.
The HVDCDC application shall support the following operating states:
Standby
Initialization
Precharge
Normal Operation
Safe State
Shutdown
Fault State
The software shall transition between states only when all safety conditions are satisfied.
The input conditions include ignition status, CAN commands, HVIL state, fault conditions, and battery voltage measurements.
The outputs include state machine status broadcasts and power module control signals.
The process flow for enabling Normal Operation is:
Verify ignition ON condition.
Verify HVIL closed condition.
Verify no active Level 2 faults.
Enable precharge path.
Verify DC link stabilization.
Enable main converter.
The pre-loaded value requires the application to boot into Standby mode.
The validation criteria require HIL testing, SIL simulation, and fault injection testing.
This requirement is considered Critical.
CAN Timing Requirement
Requirement ID

requirements.logical_block: corresponds to customer requirement SDS-EV-101 and belongs to milestone A-Sample/PF1.0.
requirements.purpose: This requirement specifies that the HVDCDC software shall support both MCAN and DCAN communication interfaces.
requirements.inputs: The inputs required for this functionality include ARXML communication configuration files provided by a third-party supplier and CAN database files for debug communication.
requirements.outputs: The outputs expected from the system include transmission of operating mode status, battery voltage, HVIL status, measured temperatures, fault levels, and current measurements over MCAN communication.
requirements.process: The process flow requires the user to apply greater than 500V on the high-voltage side, wait for voltage stabilization for 300 milliseconds, and transmit Enable command value 0x01 through MCAN.
requirements.validation: The validation criteria require verification using CANape and CANalyzer tools.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: The validation criteria require verification using CANape and CANalyzer tools.

metadata.source_section: corresponds to customer requirement SDS-EV-101 and belongs to milestone A-Sample/PF1.0.
metadata.evidence:
SWRS_COMM_001 corresponds to customer requirement SDS-EV-101 and belongs to milestone A-Sample/PF1.0.
This requirement specifies that the HVDCDC software shall support both MCAN and DCAN communication interfaces. MCAN communication shall be used for vehicle communication while DCAN communication shall be used for debugging and diagnostics.
The inputs required for this functionality include ARXML communication configuration files provided by a third-party supplier and CAN database files for debug communication.
The outputs expected from the system include transmission of operating mode status, battery voltage, HVIL status, measured temperatures, fault levels, and current measurements over MCAN communication. The DCAN interface shall broadcast internal state transitions, calibration parameters, diagnostic states, and fault identifiers.
The process flow requires the user to apply greater than 500V on the high-voltage side, wait for voltage stabilization for 300 milliseconds, and transmit Enable command value 0x01 through MCAN.
No derived requirements are applicable for this requirement. The requirement priority is Medium. No specific access restrictions are defined.
The pre-loaded val

requirements.logical_block: maps to customer requirement SDS-EV-102 and belongs to milestone PF2.0.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall support Signal Not Available handling for all CAN signals.
requirements.inputs: The input condition occurs whenever signal validation fails or signal timeout occurs.
requirements.outputs: The output behavior requires the software to transmit SNA values instead of stale data.
requirements.process: The input condition occurs whenever signal validation fails or signal timeout occurs.

metadata.source_section: maps to customer requirement SDS-EV-102 and belongs to milestone PF2.0.
metadata.evidence:
SWRS_COMM_002 maps to customer requirement SDS-EV-102 and belongs to milestone PF2.0.
The HVDCDC shall support Signal Not Available handling for all CAN signals.
For physical signals, SNA shall be represented using the maximum possible signal value. For state-based signals, SNA shall be represented using a dedicated INVALID or UNKNOWN state.
The input condition occurs whenever signal validation fails or signal timeout occurs.
The output behavior requires the software to transmit SNA values instead of stale data.
The process specifies that whenever CAN timeout exceeds 100 milliseconds, the corresponding signal shall transition into SNA state.
The default values include HVDCDCStatus equal to Standby and FaultStatus equal to No Fault.
The valid range for battery voltage signals is 200V to 950V. The valid range for current signals is -200A to 200A.
Validation activities shall be performed using CANalyzer and UART logs.
This requirement is classified as High priority and marked as critical.
Communication Requirement 3
Requirement ID

requirements.logical_block: corresponds to customer requirement SDS-EV-103 and belongs to milestone PF2.1.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC software shall receive operation commands from the vehicle CAN network.
requirements.inputs: The HVDCDC software shall receive operation commands from the vehicle CAN network.
requirements.outputs: The output behavior requires the HVDCDC state machine to transition into the requested operating state.
requirements.process: The input condition occurs whenever the vehicle broadcasts operation commands on the CAN network.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: The validation criteria require observing state transitions through UART and CAN tools.

metadata.source_section: corresponds to customer requirement SDS-EV-103 and belongs to milestone PF2.1.
metadata.evidence:
SWRS_COMM_003 corresponds to customer requirement SDS-EV-103 and belongs to milestone PF2.1.
The HVDCDC software shall receive operation commands from the vehicle CAN network.
The supported operation commands include 0x00 for Standby mode, 0x01 for Enable mode, 0x02 for Safe State mode, and 0x03 for Shutdown mode.
The input condition occurs whenever the vehicle broadcasts operation commands on the CAN network.
The output behavior requires the HVDCDC state machine to transition into the requested operating state.
The process steps are as follows:
Receive command frame from MCAN interface.
Validate CRC and rolling counter.
Validate operating conditions.
Execute requested transition.
The pre-loaded values require ignition status equal to ON and HV battery voltage greater than 450V.
The valid range for operation command signals is 0 through 3.
The validation criteria require observing state transitions through UART and CAN tools.
The requirement is feasible within all defined operating environments.
Diagnostic Requirement 1
Requirement ID

requirements.logical_block: corresponds to customer requirement SDS-EV-220 and belongs to milestone B-Sample/PF2.0.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall detect overvoltage faults on the 900V bus.
requirements.outputs: The output behavior requires the system to disable the power module, open the contactor, and broadcast fault level 0x03 over CAN.
requirements.process: Whenever the measured voltage exceeds 920V for more than 50 milliseconds, the software shall generate a Level 2 fault.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: The validation criteria require injecting simulated ADC voltages and verifying fault transitions through CAN logs.

metadata.source_section: corresponds to customer requirement SDS-EV-220 and belongs to milestone B-Sample/PF2.0.
metadata.evidence:
SWRS_DIAG_001 corresponds to customer requirement SDS-EV-220 and belongs to milestone B-Sample/PF2.0.
The HVDCDC shall detect overvoltage faults on the 900V bus.
The software shall continuously monitor the measured HV bus voltage using ADC channels.
Whenever the measured voltage exceeds 920V for more than 50 milliseconds, the software shall generate a Level 2 fault.
The output behavior requires the system to disable the power module, open the contactor, and broadcast fault level 0x03 over CAN.
The process flow is described below:
Read ADC measurement.
Compare measurement against threshold.
Validate debounce timer.
Trigger fault manager.
Broadcast fault state.
The default values include OverVoltageFault equal to FALSE and HVDCDCStatus equal to Normal.
The valid range for measured HV voltage is 300V to 950V.
The validation criteria require injecting simulated ADC voltages and verifying fault transitions through CAN logs.
This requirement is marked as Critical and High priority.
Diagnostic Requirement 2
Requirement ID

requirements.logical_block: corresponds to customer requirement SDS-EV-221 and belongs to milestone B-Sample/PF2.0.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall detect overtemperature conditions for critical power components.
requirements.inputs: The software shall trigger derating functionality whenever measured temperature exceeds 85 degrees Celsius.
requirements.outputs: The outputs include thermal derating commands, CAN fault broadcasts, and thermal warning indicators.
requirements.process: The software shall trigger derating functionality whenever measured temperature exceeds 85 degrees Celsius.
requirements.validation: The validation criteria require thermal chamber testing and sensor simulation.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: The validation criteria require thermal chamber testing and sensor simulation.

metadata.source_section: corresponds to customer requirement SDS-EV-221 and belongs to milestone B-Sample/PF2.0.
metadata.evidence:
SWRS_DIAG_002 corresponds to customer requirement SDS-EV-221 and belongs to milestone B-Sample/PF2.0.
The HVDCDC shall detect overtemperature conditions for critical power components.
The monitored components include the VICOR module, 900V EMI filter, 450V EMI filter, and solid-state switch.
The software shall trigger derating functionality whenever measured temperature exceeds 85 degrees Celsius.
The software shall trigger shutdown functionality whenever measured temperature exceeds 95 degrees Celsius.
The process requires periodic SPI ADC measurements every 10 milliseconds.
The outputs include thermal derating commands, CAN fault broadcasts, and thermal warning indicators.
The valid temperature range is -40 degrees Celsius to 125 degrees Celsius.
The validation criteria require thermal chamber testing and sensor simulation.
This requirement is categorized as High priority and Critical.

CAN Timing Requirement
Requirement ID

requirements.logical_block: corresponds to customer requirement SDS-EV-140 and belongs to milestone PF1.0.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall maintain CAN PDU cycle timing within plus or minus 2 percent of the configured cycle time.
requirements.outputs: Debug PDU shall transmit every 10 milliseconds.
requirements.validation: The validation criteria require CANoe logging and timing analysis.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: The validation criteria require CANoe logging and timing analysis.

metadata.source_section: corresponds to customer requirement SDS-EV-140 and belongs to milestone PF1.0.
metadata.evidence:
SWRS_COMM_020 corresponds to customer requirement SDS-EV-140 and belongs to milestone PF1.0.
The HVDCDC shall maintain CAN PDU cycle timing within plus or minus 2 percent of the configured cycle time.
The required PDU cycle times are listed below:
Debug PDU shall transmit every 10 milliseconds.
Vehicle Status PDU shall transmit every 20 milliseconds.
Thermal Status PDU shall transmit every 50 milliseconds.
Diagnostic Status PDU shall transmit every 100 milliseconds.
The output behavior requires all transmitted CAN messages to remain within timing tolerances.
The validation criteria require CANoe logging and timing analysis.
The requirement priority is Medium.
Sensor Interface Requirement
Requirement ID

requirements.logical_block: corresponds to customer requirement SDS-EV-410 and belongs to milestone PF3.0.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC software shall acquire voltage, current, and temperature measurements through SPI ADC interfaces.
requirements.outputs: The outputs include filtered measurement signals and diagnostic fault generation.
requirements.process: The software shall validate ADC values against configured ranges before processing.
requirements.validation: The software shall validate ADC values against configured ranges before processing.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: The validation criteria require ADC signal injection testing and oscilloscope verification.

metadata.source_section: corresponds to customer requirement SDS-EV-410 and belongs to milestone PF3.0.
metadata.evidence:
SWRS_SENSOR_001 corresponds to customer requirement SDS-EV-410 and belongs to milestone PF3.0.
The HVDCDC software shall acquire voltage, current, and temperature measurements through SPI ADC interfaces.
The ADC sampling period shall be 5 milliseconds.
The monitored signals include:
HV Bus Voltage
LV Bus Voltage
HV Bus Current
LV Bus Current
EMI Filter Temperatures
VICOR Temperatures
The software shall validate ADC values against configured ranges before processing.
The outputs include filtered measurement signals and diagnostic fault generation.
The valid voltage range is 0V to 1000V. The valid current range is -300A to 300A.
The validation criteria require ADC signal injection testing and oscilloscope verification.
OBD Requirement
Requirement ID

requirements.logical_block: corresponds to customer requirement SDS-EV-510 and belongs to milestone PF3.0.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC shall support OBD Mode 4 diagnostic requests.
requirements.inputs: The software shall receive OBD requests over CAN communication and clear stored DTCs whenever a valid Mode 4 request is received.
requirements.outputs: The software shall receive OBD requests over CAN communication and clear stored DTCs whenever a valid Mode 4 request is received.
requirements.process: The software shall receive OBD requests over CAN communication and clear stored DTCs whenever a valid Mode 4 request is received.
requirements.acceptance_criteria: The validation criteria require UDS diagnostic testing and CAN trace verification.

metadata.source_section: corresponds to customer requirement SDS-EV-510 and belongs to milestone PF3.0.
metadata.evidence:
SWRS_OBD_001 corresponds to customer requirement SDS-EV-510 and belongs to milestone PF3.0.
The HVDCDC shall support OBD Mode 4 diagnostic requests.
The software shall receive OBD requests over CAN communication and clear stored DTCs whenever a valid Mode 4 request is received.
The input condition requires valid CAN communication and authenticated diagnostic sessions.
The outputs include DTC clearing confirmation and diagnostic status updates.
The process steps include:
Receive Mode 4 request.
Validate session permissions.
Clear active DTCs.
Update nonvolatile memory.
Broadcast diagnostic status.
The validation criteria require UDS diagnostic testing and CAN trace verification.
This requirement is classified as High priority and Critical.
Non-Functional Requirement
Requirement ID

requirements.logical_block: corresponds to customer requirement SDS-EV-900 and belongs to milestone Release 1.0.
requirements.purpose: The HVDCDC software startup time shall not exceed 800 milliseconds after ignition ON.
requirements.process: The HVDCDC software startup time shall not exceed 800 milliseconds after ignition ON.
requirements.validation: Validation activities shall include performance profiling and long-duration stress testing.

metadata.source_section: corresponds to customer requirement SDS-EV-900 and belongs to milestone Release 1.0.
metadata.evidence:
SWRS_NFR_001 corresponds to customer requirement SDS-EV-900 and belongs to milestone Release 1.0.
The HVDCDC software startup time shall not exceed 800 milliseconds after ignition ON.
The software memory utilization shall remain below 85 percent of available flash memory.
The CPU utilization shall remain below 70 percent during normal operation.
The software shall support operation in ambient temperatures ranging from -40 degrees Celsius to 105 degrees Celsius.
Validation activities shall include performance profiling and long-duration stress testing.
This requirement is categorized as Medium priority.
